headlines.text: Trump announces 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, first in 34 years
Hegseth warns Iran: deal or US restarts combat as ceasefire clock ticks
Pakistan army chief in Tehran pushing for second round of US-Iran talks
S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit fresh record highs, shrugging off war jitters
AAA: national gas average hits $4.09, highest since 2022
House votes today on Iran war powers resolution to rein in Trump
Trump feud with Pope Leo XIV escalates; religious experts call it unprecedented
Lena Dunham memoir 'Famesick' drops bombshells on Adam Driver's on-set rage
Airlines warn of systemic jet fuel shortage if Strait of Hormuz stays blocked
Russia hammers Kyiv with mass drone and missile strike, killing at least 16

breaking.text: Trump announces 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire beginning 5 PM ET today

stories.hed: Hegseth Threatens Iran While Pakistan Plays Kissinger in Tehran
stories.dek: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told Iran it can have a deal or a war, which is a fun binary when your entire missile industry has been bombed into rubble. Pakistan's army chief flew to Tehran personally, because nothing says 'neutral mediator' like a country with its own nuclear weapons.

stories.hed: Russia Pounds Kyiv With Hundreds of Drones and Dozens of Missiles
stories.dek: Russia launched a mass strike on civilian areas of Ukraine, killing at least 16 people in an attack stretching for hours. The Iran war consuming global attention appears to be doing Russia's news cycle a favor.

stories.hed: House Votes Today on War Powers Resolution to Limit Trump on Iran
stories.dek: The House is set to vote on a resolution calling on Trump to halt military hostilities against Iran without a formal declaration of war. The Senate already failed to advance a similar measure, but slim House margins mean this one could actually be close.

stories.hed: Trump vs. Pope Leo XIV: A Beef for the Ages
stories.dek: Trump has been attacking Pope Leo XIV as too liberal and weak, while Leo says a 'delusion of omnipotence' is fueling the Iran war. Religious experts told NPR this level of presidential pope-bashing is completely unprecedented — and yet here we are.

stories.hed: Billionaire Ken Griffin Warns of Recession While Markets Hit Record Highs — Someone Is Wrong
stories.dek: Citadel's Ken Griffin is ringing recession alarm bells even as the S&P 500 closes above 7,000 for the first time in history. Either Griffin knows something the market doesn't, or the market is doing what it always does: aggressively not caring.

markets.context: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit fresh record highs on ceasefire optimism and strong tech earnings, with the S&P closing above 7,000 for the first time; oil climbed on Strait of Hormuz blockade fears.

gas.note: Gas hasn't been this expensive since 2022 — Iran started a war and somehow you're the one paying for it at the pump.

popCulture.hed: Lena Dunham's Memoir 'Famesick' Claims Adam Driver Hurled a Chair and Screamed in Her Face on the Set of 'Girls'
popCulture.dek: Out April 14, Dunham's explosive memoir details Adam Driver allegedly throwing a chair at the wall next to her and punching a hole in his trailer because he hated his haircut. Driver has not responded, which is somehow very on-brand for him.

genZ.definition: A TikTok carousel format where creators post slightly-off-center, mid-sentence candid shots of the person they love most — boyfriend, best friend, dog — set to a piano 'awww so cute' audio, captioned 'me when I take FB mom photos of my ___.' It went viral this week because it perfectly names the thing everyone does but never admits.
genZ.usage: The White House press team posting candid photos of Trump looking presidential during Iran negotiations is just the government version of the FB Mom Photos trend — proud, slightly awkward, and deeply invested in one person's image.

tldr: Day 48 of the Iran war brought a surprise 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire announced by Trump, a ticking US-Iran ceasefire expiring April 22, and a House war powers vote that could actually be close. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 crossed 7,000 for the first time ever and gas hit $4.09 a gallon, meaning history is being made at both Wall Street and the pump.
